---
title: "Deep Learning"
---

The building blocks of **deep learning** are foundational components and concepts that enable machines to learn from data through complex, layered models.

## Artificial Neurons

Inspired by biological neurons, an artificial neuron performs a simple computation:

$$
y = \sigma(w_1x_1 + w_2x_2 + \dots + w_nx_n + b)
$$

where $x_i$ are the inputs, $w_i$ the weights, $b$ the bias, and $\sigma$ an [activation function](activation-functions.qmd) (e.g., ReLU, sigmoid).

## Layers

- **Input Layer** — receives raw data.
- **Hidden Layers** — process data through weighted connections.
- **Output Layer** — produces the final result (e.g., class label, probability).

## Backpropagation

Algorithm for computing gradients using the chain rule to propagate the error backwards through the network. This is what makes training deep networks possible.

## Neural Network Architectures

- **Feedforward Neural Networks (FNN)** — simplest architecture, data flows in one direction
- **Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN)** — specialized for images and spatial data
- **Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN)** — designed for sequential data
- **[Transformers](transformers.qmd)** — attention-based models for NLP and beyond

## Regularization Techniques

Techniques to prevent overfitting:

- **Dropout** — randomly zeroing units during training ([Srivastava et al., 2014](https://jmlr.org/papers/v15/srivastava14a.html))
- **L2 Regularization (Weight Decay)** — penalizing large weights
- **Early Stopping** — halting training when validation loss stops improving

## Resources

- [Geoffrey Hinton's homepage](https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~hinton/)
- [fast.ai — Validation sets](https://www.fast.ai/posts/2017-11-13-validation-sets.html)
- [Imagenette dataset](https://github.com/fastai/imagenette)
- [ResNet (arXiv 1512.03385)](https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385)
- [Bag of Tricks (arXiv 1812.01187)](https://arxiv.org/abs/1812.01187)
- [minGPT](https://github.com/karpathy/minGPT) / [nanochat](https://github.com/karpathy/nanochat) by Andrej Karpathy
